chunk_summary: Guidance on replacing calcium and vitamin D in dairy-free diets, using Indian RDA targets, with dietary and supplementation strategies for children and adolescents.
learning_objectives: Understand calcium and vitamin D requirements for children and adolescents in dairy-free diets.
Identify culturally appropriate dietary sources of calcium and vitamin D in Indian contexts.
Recognize high-risk scenarios requiring supplementation over dietary counseling.
Learn monitoring strategies for growth and nutritional adequacy during elimination diets.
key_facts: Calcium RDA for children: 500 mg/day (1–3 years), 550 mg/day (4–6 years), 650 mg/day (7–9 years), 850 mg/day (10–12 years), 1000 mg/day (13–18 years).
Vitamin D RDA: 400 IU/day for infants with limited sun exposure, 600 IU/day for children and adolescents.
Culturally appropriate calcium sources include fortified foods, calcium-set tofu, ragi, and millet-based options.
High-risk groups (e.g., underweight children, food-insecure households, multifood elimination) require supplementation rather than dietary counseling alone.
Recheck growth and dietary intake within weeks of starting elimination and reassess supplementation at each visit.
reference_quote: Age-specific calcium targets commonly used in Indian RDA tables include: 500 mg/day (1–3 years), 550 mg/day (4–6 years), 650 mg/day (7–9 years), 850 mg/day (10–12 years), and 1000 mg/day (13–18 years).
document_key_topics: Calcium and vitamin D requirements in dairy-free diets
Culturally appropriate dietary replacements for calcium
Monitoring growth and nutritional adequacy during elimination diets
